hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target binding by avoiding steric clashes with SSTR3/5. This will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy produces strong binders but fails to improve selectivity over the native SST-14. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the SSTR2 pharmacophore.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 are likely to occur. Disrupting these contacts can improve selectivity without affecting the conserved pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Polar and charged residues are prioritized to reduce hydrophobic interactions with off-targets.
